title: Campe rivularis
taxonomy: Campe rivularis is a flowering plant species in the family Campanulaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Campe. Campe rivularis is native to tropical regions and is often found in moist areas. The plant is a perennial species with small, bell-shaped flowers. Campe rivularis is also known by its synonym Lobelia rivularis.
uses: Campe rivularis is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. It has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are often extracted and made into teas or tinctures for medicinal purposes. Additionally, Campe rivularis has been reported to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. More research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.

intro: Campe rivularis, also known as Camphor Weed or Rivularia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and distinctive camphor-like odor. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits in relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Campe rivularis include camphor, gentiopicrin, and other triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Campe rivularis is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations.
